paragraph: Your gait metrics have held remarkably steady this week — walking speed averaging 1.18 m/s with solid step length and asymmetry staying low most days. That consistency matters more than chasing peaks, especially when you had that strong 11,000-step day on the 28th without any mechanical fallout. Today's step count is just partial data, so the real pattern is those mid-week days where you're sustaining 4,000–5,000 steps with clean symmetry. Sleep has evened out too, hovering right around eight hours most nights, which gives your movement quality room to show up the next day.
